content.full_text:
February 16, 1978

Honorable John Garamendi  
State Senate  
State Capitol, Room 4057  
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Senator Garamendi:

This will follow-up on the July 6, 1977, letter from Michael J. Baker, Chief of the Fair Political Practices Commission's Legal Division, informing you that the Commission was going to review the disclosure of trusts under the Political Reform Act. The letter advised that you would not have to disclose your wife's interest in a revocable trust until that review was completed.

This is to advise you that the Commission, at its January 26, 1978, meeting, agreed on standards for reporting interests in trusts and authorized the staff to draft a regulation embodying those standards. Under those standards, a filer1/ of a Statement of Economic Interests who is a beneficiary of a trust (in which he or she has a 10 percent or greater interest) will have to report the interest in a trust if he or she receives income currently from the trust or if he or she has an irrevocable right to receive income or the principal at a later time. Applying those standards to the revocable trust established by your wife's mother in which your wife is named as a beneficiary, your wife's interest in that trust, assuming she does not currently receive any income, will not be a reportable interest in your Statement of Economic Interests.

The Commission will be considering a proposed regulation on the reporting of interests in trust at its

1/ In reporting investments and interests in real property, the filer must include the interests of his or her spouse and dependent children. Government Code Section 82029.

Honorable John Garamendi  
February 16, 1978  
Page Two

April 6 - 8, 1978, meeting. If you are interested in seeing this regulation or commenting on this matter, I would be happy to send you a copy of the proposed regulation, when it is available.

If you have any questions or comments, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

Barbara Campbell  
Counsel  
Legal Division

BC:plh
